title: OvaSearch
subtitle: Native C++ multimodal RAG engine
summary: A local-first multimodal retrieval engine: text, PDFs, and images flow through OpenVINO's VLM + text-embedding pipelines into a USearch HNSW index, with zero-copy image ingestion and a sliding-window chunker — all in portable C++. Selected for GSoC 2026.
body:
A retrieval-augmented generation engine that runs natively in C++, accelerated by Intel OpenVINO. Designed for low-latency multimodal search across text, image, and audio embeddings without a Python runtime in the hot path.

— BY THE NUMBERS —
embedding dim: 384
retrieval p50: ~6 ms
index: USearch HNSW
runtime: OpenVINO CPU

— WHY C++ —
Most multimodal RAG stacks assume Python and a stack of model-serving daemons. OvaSearch is a single binary that wires VLMPipeline, TextEmbeddingPipeline, and LLMPipeline from OpenVINO directly, with a custom ImageAllocator for zero-copy image ingestion. No framework tax, no Python hop — which matters when you actually run this on a laptop.

— INDEXING SHAPE —
Text and PDFs flow through a sliding-window chunker (1000 characters, 200 overlap). Images are captioned once by a Qwen2-VL pipeline and then chunked like any other document. A flat-file cache stores chunks, embeddings, and sources so cold-start reads are deterministic.

— HOT PATH (sketch) —
  // Retrieve top-k by cosine, then assemble context for the LLM
  auto query = embed(question);                    // bge-small-en-v1.5 via OpenVINO
  auto hits  = index.search(query, /*top_k=*/5);   // USearch HNSW
  for (auto& h : hits) {
    if (h.distance > 0.45f) continue;              // relevance floor
    ctx.append(store.chunk_at(h.key), store.source_of(h.key));
  }
  llm.stream(build_prompt(ctx, question));         // Llama-3.2-3B INT4

— WHAT'S NEXT —
Video + audio ingestion via keyframe sampling and whisper.cpp, hybrid lexical + semantic retrieval with BM25 + RRF, and an NPU/GPU switch for AIPC deployment. GSoC 2026 with OpenVINO is the through-line.

title: Lisper
subtitle: Local transcription studio on whisper.cpp + C++
summary: A modular C++ speech-to-text tool built on whisper.cpp. Clean API boundaries so it slots in as a tool for agentic pipelines, a library for editors, or a standalone CLI — no cloud call, no Python, no surprises.
body:
A native transcription studio wrapping whisper.cpp with a workflow for batch jobs, edit-as-you-listen review, and speaker labeling — all running on the device.

— THE IDEA —
Every transcription tool I'd tried was either a wrapper around a cloud API or a GUI hiding whisper.cpp behind Electron. Lisper is the opposite: a small, modular C++ layer on top of whisper.cpp with a clean API that can be registered as an agentic tool or called as a library.

— SHAPE —
The transcription pipeline, the model loader, and the I/O front-ends are separate compilation units, so embedders can pull exactly the pieces they need. The same codebase ships a REPL-ish CLI, a library surface, and (eventually) an editor plug-in.

— STATS —
runtime: whisper.cpp
language: C++
deps: CMake only

Built out of frustration with web-based transcription tools that ship audio off your machine. Lisper keeps everything local: drag in audio, get a clean transcript with timestamps, edit, export.
